reference_answer: 1606 Corp., a Nevada corporation.
Jefferson Enterprise Energy, LLC, a Texas limited liability company (referred to in the filing as "Seller").
The registrant is a Nevada corporation. The counterparty is a Texas limited liability company.
Angelina County, Texas. The document describes the subject as "certain real property and related assets located in Angelina County, Texas."
Austen Lambrecht, in his capacity as Chief Executive Officer.
The Date of Report (Date of earliest event reported) is April 13, 2026. The signature is dated April 16, 2026 — three days after the Date of Report.
The original Purchase and Sale Agreement was effective as of March 12, 2026.
Original closing date: April 15, 2026. New closing date under the First Amendment: May 22, 2026.
In chronological order: original Agreement effective date 03/12/2026; Date of Report 04/13/2026; original closing date 04/15/2026; signature/filing date 04/16/2026; new closing date 05/22/2026.
From April 13, 2026 through the closing date. The obligation runs from the date of the First Amendment up to (but not past) the closing.
Commission File Number 000-56467; I.R.S. Employer Identification Number 86-1497346.
All three columns — Title of each class, Trading Symbol(s), and Name of each exchange on which registered — are reported as "N/A" in the filing.
Yes. The cover page marks "Emerging growth company" as checked (☒).
Address: 2425 E. Camelback Rd Suite 150, Phoenix, AZ 85016. Telephone: (602) 481-1544.
$11,168,864. The First Amendment states the total purchase price "remains unchanged."

Before the First Amendment, the $250,000 had been deposited by the Company as earnest money. Under the First Amendment, the $250,000 is deemed fully earned by Seller as a non-refundable extension fee — given as consideration for the closing-date extension.

Yes, Seller keeps the $250,000 in both cases. The First Amendment provides that the $250,000 "shall not be credited against the purchase price at closing, regardless of whether the closing occurs or the Agreement is terminated." The fee is non-refundable in both scenarios.
No. The First Amendment expressly states that the $250,000 "shall not be credited against the purchase price at closing."
Seller must make a written request. The Company is required to provide the information only "upon Seller's written request," and only during the window from April 13, 2026 through the closing date.
37 days. April 15, 2026 to May 22, 2026 is 37 calendar days; the Amendment itself does not state this number, but it follows from the two dates the Amendment supplies.
No. The First Amendment expressly states that "The total purchase price of $11,168,864 remains unchanged."
No. The 8-K describes the $250,000 only as having been "previously deposited by the Company as earnest money"; it does not name an escrow holder, account, or instrument.
No. All four boxes (Rule 425, Rule 14a-12, Rule 14d-2(b), Rule 13e-4(c)) are shown as unchecked (☐) on the cover page.
No. The 8-K body summarizes the First Amendment but does not include or reference an Item 9.01 exhibit list, nor does it attach the First Amendment as an exhibit within the body of the filing.
No. The only description provided is "certain real property and related assets located in Angelina County, Texas." No parcel ID, acreage, or itemized list of related assets is given.
